{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft}}}} {{神社 |名称 = {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町) - DITRf35VwAANbB9.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = （伝）{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本殿 = 五間社{{ill|Ryū-zō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:New]]" to ""
revision.text:
{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft}}}} {{神社 |名称 = {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町) - DITRf35VwAANbB9.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = （伝）{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本殿 = 五間社{{ill|Ryū-zō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shrines of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushiwatatenno Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/859|qq=}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 27|qq=}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1383|qq=}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

revision.text:
{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}|{{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}}}} {{神社 |名称 = {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町) - DITRf35VwAANbB9.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} |本殿 = 五間社{{ill|Ryū-zō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryū-zō|qq=}} |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/730|qq=}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushiwatatenno Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/859|qq=}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 27|qq=}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1383|qq=}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} |本殿 = 五間社{{ill|Ryū-zō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryū-zō|qq=}} |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/730|qq=}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushiwatatenno Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/859|qq=}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 27|qq=}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1383|qq=}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

revision.text:
{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}|{{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}}}} {{神社 |名称 = {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町) - DITRf35VwAANbB9.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} |本殿 = 五間社{{ill|Ryū-zō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryū-zō|qq=}} |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/730|qq=}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushiwatatenno Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/859|qq=}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 27|qq=}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1383|qq=}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大和国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Hashihayasu Susa no Okami|lt_ja=建速須佐ノ男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Shrine|lt_ja=国魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ({{ill|730|ja|730年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/730|qq=}}) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the {{ill|Ushiwatatenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushiwatatenno Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ({{ill|859|ja|859年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/859|qq=}}), on {{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 27|qq=}}, the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ({{ill|1383|ja|1383年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1383|qq=}}) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

境内 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}
[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]]

[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]]

In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

境内 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}
[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara�]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}
