It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}|{{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}}}} {{神社 |名称 = {{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町) - DITRf35VwAANbB9.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

境内 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]三碓三丁目5-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 15.2 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 |経度秒 = 22 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（大）{{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}|prefecture= {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}}|coordinates= {{Coord|34.687555555555555|N|135.73944444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity= {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}; {{ill|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}}}}
旧{{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = （伝）[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |本殿 = 五間社[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zō]] |別名 = {{ill|Mitsuusu Shrine|ja|三碓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuusu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第三土曜日・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Nara city }} [[File:添御県坐神社 - DITRN uUMAApkMX.jpg|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}''' (Sō no Miagata Niimasu Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in the Mitsugarasu (みつがらす) 3-chōme area of [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}} and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and is one of the six {{ill|Miya|ja|御縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shrines in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. == {{nihongo|Worshipped Deities|祭神|Saijin}} == The current enshrined deities are the following three: *'''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hashihayasu Susa no Okami]]''' *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速之命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}

wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}''' *'''[[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]]''' == History == === Overview === Although the precise founding date is unclear, it is believed that the shrine originally enshrined the [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Shrine]] of the Sō no Miakata, the tutelary deity of the region, by a descendant of {{ill|Seishiroku|ja|姓氏録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishiroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1045362}} and {{ill|Old Chronicles|ja|旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀|1=Kujiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2716751}}’s account of a descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Takehaya no Mikoto|lt_ja=武乳速命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. In addition, the area surrounding the present shrine location was once a village governed by the powerful ancient 【Ono】 clan ({{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}), and it is said that the shrine was once worshipped as that clan’s [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]. The earliest historical mention appears in the Tax Ledger of Yamato Province dated to the 2nd year of the Tenpyo era ([[:en:730|730]]) where it records: “Sō no Miakata, Kobe, 152 bundles of rice stalks, 8 bunches; tax 20 bundles; total 172 bundles 8 bunches.” In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is listed among the great shrines ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Taisha|lt_ja=大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myojin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and is noted as receiving offerings during the monthly and harvest festivals ({{ill|Hohai|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hohai|qq=|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}}). During the Edo period, it was known as the [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō Shrine]]. === Shrine Rank === * In the 3rd year of the Ten'an era ([[:en:859|859]]), on [[:en:January 27|January 27]], the shrine’s Rank was raised from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the inscription being “Sō no Miakata Shrine.” == Cultural Properties == [[File:添御県坐神社本殿 - DITW7 XVwAA2ZRp.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Main Shrine Building (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Shrine Building – Constructed in the 3rd year of the Eitoku era ([[:en:1383|1383]]) and designated in the 40th year of the Meiji era ([[:en:1907|1907]]).Meiji 40, August 28, Internal Affairs Ministry Notice No.93 (see: [{{NDLDC|2950596}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]) == Sub-shrines == [[File:末社福神宮 - DITYiJmVoAE2Gbg.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Fukujingu]] * Fukujingu: Enshrines Lord Ono Fukumaro. * Eireiden * Ebisu Shrine * Amano Kagu Yama Shrine * Ryūō Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == ご参拝のしおり 富雄の里のパワースポット添御県坐神社 Visitor's Guide: Fumio no Sato Power Spot Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine == External Links == * {{commonscat-inline|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}} * [http://soumi.sub.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website) {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|version=102967769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)]] [[ja:添御県坐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sō no Miagata Niimasu jinja (Mitsugarasu, Nara)}}
